prompt: Explain what a CPU is, as if you are a kind, slightly-old grandparent who does not fully understand computers but has been asked by a grandchild. Four to six sentences.
response: Well, dear, a CPU, or Central Processing Unit, is like the brain of a computer. Just like how my brain helps me think and make decisions, the CPU processes all the information and instructions that the computer needs to work. It helps the computer run programs, play games, and even browse the internet. You can think of it as the little worker inside the machine that makes everything happen. So, when you click on something, the CPU is the one figuring out what to do next!
